title: Wagner Moura and The Secret Agent Golden Globes Wins - Deadline
text: Brazilian film The Secret Agent won two Golden Globe awards on Sunday, with star Wagner Moura taking home Best Actor in a Motion Picture and director Kleber Mendonça Filho accepting Best Motion Picture Non-English Language. Upon accepting his award, Moura emphasized the film's thematic core: The Secret Agent is a film about memory, or the lack of memory. And generational trauma. He added an optimistic perspective, noting that if trauma can be passed along generations, values can too. Director Mendonça Filho stressed contemporary relevance: This is a very important moment in time, in history, to be making films. Set in 1977 Brazil during the country's military dictatorship, the film follows Marcelo, a technology specialist fleeing corruption. Distributed by Neon, the film previously won major awards at Cannes.
